untranslatable_reason: BARR pattern identification requires multi-bar formation analysis, trend line establishment, and lead-in height calculation. These require pattern recognition logic not expressible with single-bar price indicators and standard technical indicators. Specifically: (1) 'BARR formation identified' requires detecting a specific bar pattern formation over multiple bars; (2) 'trend line established' requires trend line fitting across multiple points; (3) 'Lead-in height calculated from first quarter of formation' requires measuring the vertical distance between highest high and trend line within a specific section of the pattern. Standard OHLC, MA, RSI, ATR, and other allowed indicators cannot express these pattern-specific geometric measurements.
translation_notes: The BARR Measure Rule is a proprietary chart pattern recognition method that relies on visual pattern identification and geometric measurement of formation components. It cannot be reliably expressed using only standard technical indicators. A proper implementation would require: (a) pattern recognition engine to identify BARR formations, (b) trend line solver to fit and validate trend lines, (c) multi-bar lookback to measure lead-in height across the first quarter of formation. The entry/exit conditions provided are placeholder reversals and should not be used for live trading without implementing the full BARR pattern detection logic.
